steps: Add 5.0 g Yeast Extract to a 1 Liter Erlenmeyer Flask. undetermined Yeast Extract Contributed by users Catalog #Y1625
Add 30 g Glucose or Dextrose to the flask. undetermined Glucose P212121 Catalog #Glucose
Add 0.25 g Adenine to the flask. undetermined Adenine Contributed by users Catalog #A8626
Add 0.25 g Uracil to the flask. undetermined Uracil Contributed by users Catalog #U0750
Add 0.25 g Histidine to the flask. undetermined L-Histidine monohydrochloride monohydrate Contributed by users Catalog #H8125
Add 0.25 Leucine to the flask. undetermined L-Leucine Contributed by users Catalog #L8000
Add 0.25 g Lysine to the flask. undetermined L-Lysine Contributed by users Catalog #L5626
Add 1 g Arginine to the flask. undetermined L-Arginine Contributed by users Catalog #A5006
Optional: For solid medium add 20 g Difco Bacto Agar to the flask. undetermined Difco Bacto Agar Carolina Catalog #156783B
Fill the flask with water up to the 1 Liter mark on the flask. undetermined
Place a magnetic stir bar into the flask and place the flask on a magnetic stirrer to dissolve the powder into the water. 00:10:00
Remove the stir bar with a magnetic rod.
Place aluminum foil over the top of the flask and then autoclave on a liquid cycle for 20 minutes. 01:00:00

If solid medium, pour while still hot into plates and let them sit out to cool overnight on the lab bench. If liquid medium, aliquot into smaller amounts and store at + 4 C. Solid medium must be poured while still hot, otherwise it can solidify inside the flask. Storing liquid medium in small aliquots helps prevent against widespread contamination through normal use. After solid medium-filled plates cool, store inverted at +4 C.
creator: Alan Cone
